summary:
The Area Under the Curve (AUC) measures total drug exposure by integrating plasma concentrations over time, while \(C_{max}\), though indicating the peak concentration, does not account for when it was reached; thus AUC is essential for understanding complete pharmacokinetics. \(\)

The other options do not provide comprehensive data regarding both magnitude and duration of effect as accurately as AUC can convey through integration across all sampled times post-drug administration.\)
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning:

- \(C_{max}\) indicates the peak plasma concentration but doesn't reflect the timing or duration.
- AUC integrates plasma concentrations over time, providing an accurate measurement of total drug exposure.

Therefore, 
\[ \boxed{\text{B}} \] corresponds to measuring the integral of plasma concentration over time—i.e., AUC—which best captures the rate of drug absorption fully.

Therefore,
